title: marsdenia roylei
intro: Marsdenia roylei, also known as the climbing milkvine, is a plant species native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, Marsdenia roylei is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or extracts, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
